steps.title: Factor the expression
steps.content: Factor $6x^8y^2 + 12x^2y^2$ by pulling out the greatest common factor. Both terms share $6x^2y^2$: $6x^8y^2 + 12x^2y^2 = 6x^2y^2(x^6 + 2)$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $6x^2y^2(2x^6)$: Expanding gives $12x^8y^2$, which is missing the $12x^2y^2$ term entirely. ✗
  • Choice B — $6x^2y^2(x^4)$: Expanding gives $6x^6y^2$, which matches neither term of the original expression. ✗
  • Choice C — $6x^2y^2(x^6+2)$: Expanding gives $6x^8y^2 + 12x^2y^2$ — exactly the original expression. ✓
  • Choice D — $6x^2y^2(x^4+2)$: Expanding gives $6x^6y^2 + 12x^2y^2$, but the original has $x^8$, not $x^6$. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
